career_angle: This is why you don't pick models based on vendor testimonials. Seek independent benchmarks (LMSYS Chatbot Arena, academic papers, or your own evals on representative tasks) before investing engineering time.

dimensions.name: Source Authority
dimensions.rationale: Fireworks is a real, funded inference company, but this is their own blog promoting their own platform—automatic credibility discount for promotional content.

dimensions.name: Evidence Quality
dimensions.rationale: Two testimonials + one unsubstantiated metric (90% vs 62%) with zero methodology, benchmark link, or test definition. No data, no papers, no reproducibility documentation.

dimensions.name: Reproducibility
dimensions.rationale: Impossible to verify. No details on test scope, what 'error-free generation' means, model versions, or inference setup. Could be a narrow task Fireworks optimizes for.

dimensions.name: Conflict of Interest
dimensions.rationale: Maximum conflict of interest: Fireworks promoting their own product on their blog. Vercel is a paying customer/partner, not an independent third party.

dimensions.name: Consensus
dimensions.rationale: Fireworks has decent industry credibility, but this specific claim (beating Sonnet by 28 percentage points) contradicts broader benchmarks (LMSYS, Chatbot Arena) showing Sonnet as highly competitive.

dimensions.name: Specificity
dimensions.rationale: The 90% vs 62% claim looks specific but is actually vague—no definition of 'error-free generation', no task disclosure, no model version clarity. Specific numbers masking vague substance.

dimensions.name: Track Record
dimensions.rationale: Fireworks is a real company with real products, but this particular performance claim is unvetted. Marketing claims ≠ audited benchmarks.
